signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the directional edge is still limited. Macro is still RED, so it does not earn fresh size.
ETH is mildly constructive with slightly supportive funding, but the board still lacks enough edge to justify a fresh position.
SOL is one of the better majors here with improved funding and decent price action, but not strong enough to rank above the better squeeze names.

TNSR remains one of the strongest continuation longs on the board with strong 24h expansion, negative funding, and a bullish trade flag. Macro still being RED keeps this at reduced-size conviction only.
CHILLGUY has strong momentum, but the market is too thin, too wide, and too funding-rich on the long side to trust as a fresh trade.
DASH has momentum and decent volume, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Watchlist only.
VVV has real strength and reasonable liquidity, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not best-in-class.
ZEC has one of the better setups on the board with strong momentum, negative funding, real liquidity, and a bullish trade flag. It is already somewhat extended, so I would keep size modest.
MON has become momentum-positive and no longer offers a clean bearish asymmetry. It does not stand out enough on either side here.
KAITO remains the clearest bearish setup with a large negative 24h move, positive funding, and a bearish mechanical profile. It is still thin, so conviction stays moderate.
BLUR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically with decent volume and negative funding, but the move is less clean than TNSR and the spread is wider.
DYM has a real squeeze profile with negative funding and bullish mechanics, but the market is thin and execution quality is weaker than the top setups.
GRASS has strength, but the board is still neutral and the spread is wide. It is not a top-tier entry.
FARTCOIN has already suffered a large downside move and the funding structure is mixed, which weakens the short case. Not clean enough either way.
ZEREBRO has momentum, but liquidity is thin and funding is positive. Too unstable to rank highly.
ARB has a decent secondary long profile with improving funding and okay liquidity, but still not stronger than ZEC or TNSR.
WLFI has some reversal ingredients with negative funding and crowded positioning, but the board remains mixed and not clean enough for a fresh trade.
TAO remains a reasonable bearish lean with a negative 24h profile and a bearish mechanical board read. The edge is not huge, but short still looks better than long.
PYTH has acceptable liquidity and increasingly supportive funding, making it an interesting secondary long, but it still does not rank ahead of the strongest setups.
MINA still has one of the strongest squeeze-continuation profiles with deeply negative funding and bullish mechanics, but the market is very thin and the spread is ugly, so size should stay small.
